scope_note: QA Orbit Access Theorem Cert family [279]. Primary source for Pisano-period framing: Wall, D. D. (1960), Fibonacci series modulo m, American Mathematical Monthly 67(6), 525-532. DOI: 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541. Canonical orbit classifier: qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family on (b, e, m), commit e7b2af0. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for mod-9 route enumeration — all pairs (b, e) with b+2e=a, b>=1, e>=1, A1-reduced via bq=((b-1)%9)+1, eq=((e-1)%9)+1 — the set of orbit classes reachable as a varies is determined exclusively by gcd(a, 3): (1) gcd(a,3)=1 (a coprime to 3): all routes are Cosmos, zero Satellite, zero Singularity; (2) 3|a and 9 not divides a: routes include Cosmos and Satellite, zero Singularity; (3) 9|a: routes include Cosmos, Satellite, and Singularity. Verified on corpus: coprime_to_3={8,13,28,50}, mul_3_not_9={12,15,21}, mul_9={9,27,126,144}. Algebraic mechanism: Satellite period=8=Pisano(3); Cosmos period=24=Pisano(9); divisibility by 3 (resp. 9) governs which Pisano orbit classes are reachable. Application domains verified: nuclear magic numbers (a=126 uniquely has Sat+Sing among {8,20,28,50,82,126,184}); Fibonacci (F_12=144 first with Sing access); FST/Briddell (all powers of 3 and proton a=1836 in mul_9 class). Cert does NOT claim: unique (b,e) pairs per orbit; exact route proportions per class; Satellite fraction exactly 2/9 (convergent only asymptotically); any claim about Pythagorean triple F values. Checks OAT_1/OAT_2/OAT_3/SRC/F; 6 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok.
